_meta.description: BeeDev Services — maintenance pages and notice registry

usage.maintenance_site: Reads 'pages' entries to render branded maintenance pages. Key by page slug (e.g. 'noveleshelf', 'noveleshelf-emergency').
usage.notice_banner: React component in each client Vite app reads its own entry by client_id key. Only reads its own entry — 'beedev-services' being active has no effect on 'noveleshelf'.

field_reference.is_active: true = banner shows and/or maintenance page is live. false = everything off, entry is ignored.
field_reference.status: Controls banner color and icon. Values: 'scheduled' (yellow/warning), 'maintenance' (blue/info), 'emergency' (red/danger).
field_reference.show_from: ISO 8601 UTC datetime. Banner will not appear before this time. Also used to calculate dismiss tier (see dismiss_tiers). Set to null to show immediately with no tier logic (12hr dismiss hold used as default).
field_reference.expires_at: ISO 8601 UTC datetime. Banner auto-hides after this time. Set to null to disable auto-hide — you must manually flip is_active to false.
field_reference.dismissible: true = user can dismiss the banner. false = banner is permanent (use for emergencies). Note: even when true, dismiss is automatically locked when under 6 hours from show_from.
field_reference.estimatedReturn: Plain text string shown in the banner and on the maintenance page. e.g. 'Friday at 4:00 PM EST'. Only shown if showEstimatedReturn is true.
field_reference.lastUpdated: ISO 8601 datetime. For your reference only — not read by any app.
field_reference.updatedBy: Name string. For your reference only — not read by any app.
field_reference.pre_message: Shown in the banner BEFORE show_from is reached. Use this for advance notice: e.g. 'Scheduled maintenance on Friday Jun 12 at 4:00 AM EST. The site will be briefly unavailable.' Falls back to 'message' if not set or if show_from is null/passed.
field_reference.maint_at: ISO 8601 UTC datetime of the actual maintenance window. Used to: (1) switch banner from pre_message to message, (2) calculate dismiss tiers. show_from controls when the banner first appears; maint_at is when things actually go down. Set to null for emergencies where there is no scheduled time.

dismiss_tiers.explanation: When show_from is set, the dismiss button behavior tightens automatically as maintenance approaches. When show_from is null, the 12hr default is always used.
dismiss_tiers.more_than_24hrs_away: User can dismiss — banner stays hidden for 12 hours, then reappears.
dismiss_tiers.6_to_24hrs_away: User can dismiss — banner stays hidden for 2 hours, then reappears.
dismiss_tiers.under_6hrs_away: Dismiss button is hidden entirely. Banner cannot be cleared.

utc_offset_reference.explanation: show_from and expires_at must be in UTC. Convert from US timezones:
utc_offset_reference.ET_summer_EDT: Add 4 hours. e.g. Friday 4:00 AM ET = Friday 08:00 UTC = '2026-06-12T08:00:00Z'
utc_offset_reference.ET_winter_EST: Add 5 hours. e.g. Friday 4:00 AM ET = Friday 09:00 UTC = '2026-01-09T09:00:00Z'

planned_maintenance: 3. Set show_from to when banner should start showing (UTC)
4. Set expires_at to when banner should auto-hide after maintenance ends (UTC)
5. Set estimatedReturn to plain text e.g. 'Friday at 4:00 PM EST' and showEstimatedReturn to true
6. When site goes down, update .htaccess to redirect to maintenance site
7. When site comes back up, remove .htaccess redirect and set is_active to false
emergency: 1. Set is_active to true on the emergency entry (e.g. 'noveleshelf-emergency')
3. Leave show_from and expires_at as null — manage manually
4. Update .htaccess redirect if site is actually down
5. When resolved, set is_active to false and remove .htaccess redirect

emergency-default.headline: We're working on it.
emergency-default.message: Something needs our attention behind the scenes. Our hive is actively investigating the issue and working to restore service as quickly as possible.

beedev-services.headline: Our hive is getting a tune-up.
beedev-services.message: We're making updates behind the scenes to improve your experience. Thank you for your patience while we work.

noveleshelf.headline: The shelves are getting a quick refresh.
noveleshelf.pre_message: The site will be unavailable on Friday Jun 12 at 4:00 AM EST for scheduled updates or maintenance.
noveleshelf.message: We're making scheduled updates behind the scenes to improve the reading and author experience.

noveleshelf-emergency.headline: NoveleShelf is temporarily unavailable.
noveleshelf-emergency.message: We're aware of an issue affecting the platform and are actively working on a resolution.

custom-default.headline: We'll be right back.
custom-default.message: Updates are currently in progress. Thank you for your patience while improvements are being made.

inactive-default.headline: This site appears to be active.
inactive-default.message: The maintenance notice is currently turned off. You can head back to the website using the button below.
